tagline: Enhanced performance and reduced storage for Delta Lake tables.
what_it_does: Parquet v2 introduces advanced encodings, v2 data page headers, and `INT64` timestamps for Delta Lake tables, significantly improving query performance and reducing storage requirements. Users can activate this feature by setting the `delta.parquet.format.version` table property to `2.12.0` or by enabling automatic upgrades. This functionality is supported in Databricks Runtime 18.1 and above.
why_it_matters: The introduction of Parquet v2 allows organizations to optimize their data storage and enhance query performance, which can lead to reduced costs and improved operational efficiency. This is particularly beneficial for data-intensive applications and analytics workloads, enabling faster insights and better resource utilization.

capabilities.title: Advanced Data Encoding
capabilities.desc: Utilizes advanced encodings to enhance data compression and performance.

capabilities.title: Improved Query Performance
capabilities.desc: Reduces query execution time through optimized data page headers.

capabilities.title: Automatic Upgrades
capabilities.desc: Enables seamless upgrades to new features without manual intervention.

capabilities.title: Compatibility Verification
capabilities.desc: Ensures workload compatibility before enabling new features on existing tables.

prerequisites: Unity Catalog managed tables in Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg formats
Serverless compute must be available in your region
use_cases: Optimizing storage costs for large datasets.
Enhancing performance for data analytics applications.
Seamlessly upgrading existing Delta Lake tables to leverage new features.
Improving data governance and management with automatic compatibility checks.
limitations: Tables shared via Delta Lake Sharing are excluded from automatic upgrades.
No batch rollback mechanism for turning off features across all tables.
Materialized views and streaming tables are not supported.
Path-based access workloads are not tracked by automatic upgrades.

steps.title: Enable Automatic Upgrades
steps.desc: Activate automatic upgrades for new schemas to utilize Parquet v2 features.

steps.title: Monitor Table Compatibility
steps.desc: Check table access patterns to ensure compatibility with automatic upgrades.

steps.title: Review Upgrade History
steps.desc: Use the Catalog Explorer to verify if automatic upgrades have been applied.

steps.title: Manage Features
steps.desc: Utilize SQL commands to turn off features if necessary.

key_takeaway: Parquet v2 for Delta Lake tables enhances performance and reduces storage costs, enabling organizations to optimize their data management strategies.
